segments.text: I am sorry. Those were the words of Azar Askin, the man who invented one of the most powerful feature in the digital
history. In 2006, Aza created infinite scroll. Inspired by the bottomless pole of snacks. It was meant to make browsing
smoother to remove friction. But what started as a thoughtful design became a force bigger than its creator. Platforms
adopted it everywhere, turning passive browsing into endless pursuit. It wasn't just smoother. Infinite scroll tapped
into deep human psychology. Intermittent rewards, variable patterns, the same principles that makes a slot machine
addictive. Each swipe promise the possibility of something new, something better. Infinite Scroll was designed for
ease, but let's face it, we are terrible at knowing when to stop. If you like this, follow for more such amazing design content.
